Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Task: Constrained Rectangular Packing Optimization. Objective: Maximize the total satisfaction score of N advertisers by placing non-overlapping axis-aligned rectangles within a bounded 2D plane. 1. Mathematical Definition: Domain: A square area S = [0, 10000] x [0, 10000]. Input Data: A set of N advertisers, where each advertiser i is defined by: A required point Pi = (xi, yi) in S. A target area Ai > 0. Decision Variables: For each advertiser i, define a rectangle Ri by its bounds [xi,min, xi,max] x [yi,min, yi,max] such that: 0 <= xi,min < xi,max <= 10000 and 0 <= yi,min < yi,max <= 10000. Objective Function: Maximize Z = sum of Score(Ri, Pi, Ai), where Score is a function of how closely the rectangle's area |Ri| matches Ai and whether Pi is in Ri. Assume Score_i = exp(-|Area(Ri) - Ai| / Ai) if Pi is in Ri, and 0 otherwise. 2. Constraints: Hard Constraint 1 (Containment): For every i, xi,min <= xi <= xi,max and yi,min <= yi <= yi,max. Hard Constraint 2 (Non-overlapping): For all i != j, the interior of Ri and Rj do not intersect. Hard Constraint 3 (Boundary): Ri is a subset of S for all i. 3. Implementation Protocol: Step 1: Initial Greedy Placement: Sort advertisers by target area Ai (descending order). For each advertiser, attempt to place a rectangle centered at Pi with area Ai. If the rectangle overlaps with previously placed rectangles or exceeds boundaries, shrink the rectangle towards Pi until it is valid or becomes infinitesimally small.
